$schema_note: F0-009 machine-readable tenant/model inventory. Documentation-only evidence artifact. Does not authorize any implementation. See docs/architecture/tenant-rls-pgbouncer-poc-design.md for narrative design.

generated_from.method: Full manual read of server/prisma/schema.prisma (2994 lines, all 91 model blocks read directly, not sampled) plus targeted grep/Read of server/src/db.ts, server/src/utils/clinicScope.ts, server/src/middleware/clinicAccess.ts, server/src/utils/tenantGuard.ts, server/src/routes/platformAdmin.ts, server/src/jobs/*.ts, and raw-SQL call sites.

classification_legend.platform_global: No tenant column of any kind; global/system entity. Never filtered by clinicId/organizationId.
classification_legend.organization_scoped: Has organizationId directly, no clinicId column (shared across an org's clinics).
classification_legend.clinic_scoped_direct: Has clinicId directly, no organizationId column on this model.
classification_legend.clinic_scoped_dual_key: Has both clinicId and organizationId as direct columns (Phase 1b dual-key pattern).
classification_legend.org_scoped_optional_clinic: organizationId required, clinicId nullable (unresolved-clinic states, e.g. inbox entries before staff triage).
classification_legend.child_via_parent: No own tenant column; scope must be derived by joining to a parent row that itself carries clinicId/organizationId.
classification_legend.ambiguous_nullable_tenant: organizationId AND clinicId both nullable by explicit design (some rows have no resolvable tenant, e.g. platform-admin-only security events) — naive RLS predicate would either hide legitimate system rows or leak them; requires an explicit design decision, not a default policy.

guard_mode_legend.auto_filter_clinicId: Prisma guard/extension can safely auto-inject where.clinicId = ctx.clinicId (or IN ctx.allowedClinicIds) for reads and validate on writes.
guard_mode_legend.auto_filter_dual_key: Guard injects both clinicId and organizationId predicates; write-payload validation must confirm the two are mutually consistent (clinic.organizationId === payload.organizationId).
guard_mode_legend.auto_filter_org_optional_clinic: Guard injects organizationId always; clinicId predicate is applied only when ctx has selected/default clinic and the row's clinicId is non-null — reads with unresolved clinicId remain org-visible by design.
guard_mode_legend.parent_ownership_validation: No column to filter on directly; guard must post-query-validate (or pre-write-validate) that the referenced parent row belongs to ctx's tenant, since a naive nested include/connect could reach across tenants via the parent FK.
guard_mode_legend.system_context_required: Must never be reachable under a normal tenant request context; guard must fail closed unless ctx.mode === 'system' or 'platform_admin' with an audited reason.
guard_mode_legend.manual_review_required: Nullable-tenant ambiguity means no default guard mode is safe to assume; needs an explicit PoC decision (see design doc §C/§D) before any guard rule is written.

rls_policy_family_legend.direct_column_policy: USING (clinic_id = current_setting('app.clinic_id')) or organization_id equivalent — cheapest, no join.
rls_policy_family_legend.dual_key_policy: USING (organization_id = ... AND clinic_id = ...) — belt-and-suspenders, still no join.
rls_policy_family_legend.parent_derived_join_policy: USING (EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM parent_table WHERE parent_table.id = this.parent_id AND parent_table.clinic_id = ...)) — join cost must be measured in the PoC.
rls_policy_family_legend.policy_function: A SECURITY DEFINER helper function encapsulates the predicate (e.g. for org_scoped_optional_clinic's conditional logic) so the policy expression stays simple and testable in isolation.
rls_policy_family_legend.system_bypass_only: Table is not tenant-filtered at all; RLS (if enabled for defense-in-depth) only distinguishes system/migrator role (BYPASSRLS) from app role (deny-all or explicit allow), never a per-tenant predicate.
rls_policy_family_legend.not_applicable_no_rls: Platform-global table; RLS is not a meaningful control here — access control stays at the application/platform-admin-auth layer.

summary_counts.note_on_counts: Correction (F0-009 correction pass, 2026-07-19): this block previously listed stale, internally-inconsistent counts (47/22/7/5/6/5/3, summing to 95) left over from an earlier classification draft. These figures are recomputed by counting the classification field of each of the 91 model entries above (deterministic script, zero manual entry); they sum to 91 and match schema.prisma's 91 models exactly. No model's classification field was changed to produce this correction. SecuritySignalEvent/SecurityIncident/CommunicationConsentConflictBucket/MessagingInboundEvent are the 4 ambiguous_nullable_tenant entries; SecurityIncidentActivity inherits that ambiguity but is counted under child_via_parent (no own tenant column).

server/src/services/security/securityIncidentService.ts:138 ($executeRawUnsafe, id-only WHERE — upstream tenant-ownership check not verified in this pass)

confidence_note: All 91 models were read directly from server/prisma/schema.prisma in this task; classification confidence is 'high' except CommunicationConsentConflictBucket and MessagingInboundEvent (medium — schema read directly but the surrounding service/ingestion code that resolves or backfills their tenant keys was not traced in this pass). This inventory is a design-time artifact; it has not been validated against a running database or an actual query-plan/index analysis, which is explicitly deferred to the disposable RLS PoC described in the main design document.
